original_abstract: Chiral nanographenes represent a captivating frontier in polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) research, merging the structural elegance of inherent chirality with the exceptional electronic, optical, and magnetic properties of graphene-like frameworks. These non-planar, contorted architectures—ranging from twisted acenes to complex helicene hybrids—offer a versatile platform for the development of next-generation nanoelectronic and optoelectronic materials. Precise control over their chiroptical properties, such as circular dichroism (CD) and circularly polarized luminescence (CPL), is increasingly achievable through rational synthetic design. This review highlights recent and significant advancements in the synthesis of chiral nanographenes, with a particular emphasis on innovative strategies beyond conventional Scholl chemistry. We explore emerging classes including expanded and $\pi$-extended helicenes, imide-functionalized nanostructures, multiple helicenes, and nonalternant systems containing non-hexagonal rings. By detailing the synthesis, configurational stability, and unique physical behavior of these molecules, this work aims to illustrate how structural contortion and symmetry breaking drive the discovery of novel materials for spintronics and advanced electronics.
humanized_abstract: Chiral nanographenes represent a newly explored class of Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) that combine the appealing inherent chirality phenomenon with the exciting electronic, optical and magnetic properties of graphene analogues. Nonplanar chiral forms such as, but not limited to, contorted acenes and helicene–graphene hybrids have attracted large interest in terms of potential uses in future nanoelectronics and nanooptronics. Progress on control of their chiroptical behavior including CD and CPL responses has been achieved. This article discusses recent advances in the syntheses of chiral nanographenes emphasizing strategies other than traditional Scholl reactions. New architectures such as expanded and π-expanded helicenes, imide-based nanographenes and multiple helices as well as nonalternant PAHs comprising non-hexagonal rings have emerged. The present review covers as much as possible the synthesis, conformational stability, and associated unusual physical properties associated with such novel chiral systems. It is demonstrated here that by tuning the extent of contortion together with different aspects of materials science related to spintronics and electronics have been opened.
